key_quote: The verification chain has a critical gap between the Lean 4 formalization and the Rust implementation: there is no verified extraction, reflection, or formal correspondence connecting the proved theorems to the executed code
rationale: All five reviewers recognize the novelty of contamination-certified evaluation certificates and WFA over semirings as the bridging abstraction. The core idea is acknowledged as architecturally insightful. The primary blockers are verification depth gaps (Lean-Rust semantic gap, deferred must-prove proofs), the semi-honest security assumption contradicting the adversarial threat model, and engineering scale risk. If execution were perfected, novelty would not be questioned. This is Category B: novel idea, needs deeper rigor and complete verification.
